name: Public Form Application
description: A complete citizen-facing step-by-step form wizard for government services. Guides users through multi-step applications with clear progress indication, validation, review, and confirmation pages.
context: Citizens applying for government services, benefits, permits, registrations, or submitting requests to government agencies
whenToUse: Citizen-facing benefit applications (child benefits, disability support, housing assistance)
Permit and license applications (business licenses, building permits, hunting licenses)
Government requests and submissions (access to information, feedback, complaints)
Any multi-step process requiring guided completion from public users

designPrinciples.oneQuestionPerPage: Break complex forms into single, focused questions to reduce cognitive load
designPrinciples.progressiveDisclosure: Show only what's needed at each step, avoid overwhelming users
designPrinciples.plainLanguage: Write at grade 9 reading level, avoid jargon and government-speak
designPrinciples.errorPrevention: Validate early and often, prevent errors before they happen
designPrinciples.saveProgress: Allow users to save and return later for longer forms
designPrinciples.clearNextSteps: Always tell users what happens next and when

appStructure.layout: Single column centered content with progress indicator
appStructure.navigation: Linear with back navigation, no side menu for focused completion
appStructure.state: Form context for cross-step data persistence and validation

pages.name: Start Page
pages.description: Service entry point with requirements, time estimate, and get started button

pages.name: Question Steps
pages.description: Individual form steps with one or few related questions per page

pages.name: Review Page
pages.description: Summary of all answers with ability to change before submission

pages.name: Confirmation Page
pages.description: Success message with reference number and next steps

questions.label: What is your full legal name?
What is your date of birth?
What is your Social Insurance Number (SIN)?
questions.helpText: Your 9-digit SIN is on your SIN card, tax return, or Record of Employment

questions.label: How would you prefer we contact you?
Are you a resident of Alberta?
How long have you lived in Alberta?
Do you have children under 18 years old?
How many children under 18 do you have?
I declare that the information provided is true and complete to the best of my knowledge.
I have read and agree to the terms and conditions.

form-page.purpose: Main form page container with proper content width

form-header.purpose: Header section with progress and back navigation

review-item.purpose: Individual answer display on review page

change-link.purpose: Link to change answers on review page

error-summary.purpose: Validation error summary at top of page

formUtils.getStepById: Get step configuration by step ID
formUtils.getNextStep: Get next step in sequence, handling conditionals
formUtils.isStepComplete: Check if all required fields in step are filled
formUtils.formatFieldValue: Format field values for display (dates, phones, etc.)

validationUtils.validateStep: Validate all fields in current step, return errors object
validationUtils.validateField: Validate single field against its rules
validationUtils.getFieldError: Get human-readable error message for field

storageUtils.saveProgress: Save form data to localStorage for later continuation
storageUtils.clearProgress: Clear saved form data after submission
storageUtils.hasExistingProgress: Check if user has saved progress to resume

dateUtils.calculateAge: Calculate age from date of birth
dateUtils.isValidDateOfBirth: Check if DOB is reasonable (not future, not too old)

FormContext.purpose: Manage form state across all steps

example.description: Complete public form application with routing, validation, and state management

src/.App.tsx: Main app with routing, header, footer, and providers
src/.App.css: Global styles including form page utility classes
src/.index.tsx: Entry point with React DOM render

routes/.StartPage.tsx: Service entry with requirements and get started

considerations: Use GoabOneColumnLayout for proper content width and centering
Include GoabMicrositeHeader with pre-release badge for beta services
Implement save progress to localStorage for forms taking >10 minutes
Show clear progress indicator (Step X of Y) on every step
Use 'Save and continue' button text, not just 'Next'
Provide back navigation on every step except start page
Validate on continue, not on every keystroke (prevents frustration)
Show error summary at top of page with anchor links to fields
Use plain language - Grade 9 reading level maximum
Include helpful context text for complex questions (SIN, postal code)
Display 'Not provided' for optional unanswered questions on review
Generate reference number in format SERVICE-YEAR-NNNNNN
Provide print option on confirmation page
Include clear next steps and contact information on confirmation
accessibilityRequirements: All form fields must have visible labels (GoabFormItem)
Error messages must be associated with fields via aria-describedby
Error summary must be focusable and announced to screen readers
Progress indicator must be announced (aria-live or heading)
Back link must have clear purpose without visual context
Radio groups must have fieldset/legend structure
Focus must move to first error field on validation failure
Keyboard navigation must work throughout entire form
Color must not be only indicator of errors (use icons, text)
Touch targets must be minimum 44x44 pixels

wireframeMode.description: This template supports wireframe mode for early-stage validation. Press Cmd+. (Mac) or Ctrl+. (Windows) to toggle between production and wireframe appearance.
wireframeMode.purpose: Use wireframe mode when showing form flows to stakeholders to get feedback on step structure and question flow, not visual polish.
wireframeMode.toggle: Keyboard shortcut Cmd+. / Ctrl+. toggles wireframe mode
